programmes.show_description: House Of Cards: The team find themselves at the centre of a dangerous rescue mission involving a Russian defector. Can the team identify and defeat the sleeper agents before it's too late?
Split!: All clues point to a notorious foreign agent when members of the secret service are murdered. The only problem is that Steed killed the prime suspect five years ago.
Kim: A wounded five-year-old Korean boy whose parents are lost captures the hearts of the 4077th, particularly Trapper who, after consulting his wife, wants to adopt him.
L.I.P.: Corporal Walker is being sent home, and he wants to marry his Korean girlfriend so she and their baby can return with him. Hawkeye promises to start the paperwork rolling.
The Trial of Henry Blake: Henry's on trial and Hawkeye and Trapper are put under house arrest to prevent them from showing up with evidence exonerating Henry.
Paper Chase: Henry is concerned about Matthew's lack of effort towards his upcoming exams.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Gift Of Life: An immoral business tycoon, who shares no love with the world or anybody in it, is given the opportunity to atone for the pollution and poverty he's been inflicting.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Man's Best Friend: Part 1: Jonathan and Mark find a dog and take him over to the orphanage to help cheer up the kids.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Fight For Your Life: A trainer is reminded of his own shady past when a fighter he's been mentoring is asked to take a fall.
The People Next Door: The people of Mission Estates don't know that one of their own, a doctor, is hiding a secret. Now Jonathan and Mark must help him confront his past.
The Wild Boy: Part 2: Dr McQueen learns of young Matthew's whereabouts and seeks custody of him so that he can use him for his own personal gain. Part 2 of 2.
The Return Of Nellie: Nellie comes home for a visit and discovers that Nancy is more of a spoilt brat than ever.
The Empire Builders: The railroad is coming to Walnut Grove, and with it the promise of jobs and economic growth. But that joy turns sour when the townspeople learn the side effects.
The Search: Olivia and two of her children become lost on Walton Mountain, while Grandma keeps Erin and Mary Ellen busy doing chores all day.
The Secret: Young Jim-Bob Walton searches for the truth about his birth and finds it shrouded in mystery.
Lost Sheep: The appearance of a young stranger unsettles some of the older residents of the village. Siobhain gets some surprising news.
Lost Souls: Tom attempts to reunite a 6-year-old girl with her family after she is found abandoned on the steps of the hospital. Meanwhile, Phyllis is convinced that her home is haunted.
Private Lives: Dr Finlay suffers some embarrassment when his mother comes to visit. He also clashes with Dr Napier over her treatment of Annie Kirk, who is suffering from a heart condition.
Rumpole and the Children of the Devil: Rumpole defends the Timson family yet again - but this time in family court. Can Rumpole overturn social services decision to take a girl into care?
Deal Me Out: Radar hits a local who is famous for jumping in front of vehicles for the compensation. Captain Pak arrives at the 4077th for a marathon poker game disguised as a conference.
Hot Lips and Empty Arms: Hot Lips receives a letter from home causing her to reassess her life; she learns her friend has married a doctor and now has a beautiful home, a pool, and children.
Officers Only: In gratitude for Hawkeye and Trapper saving his son, a general donates an officers' club to the 4077th with negative consequences. Klinger pretends to be pregnant.
Jacko feels the Italian way of life is not for him, and he continues to search for the perfect woman.
Gold-digging Paulette tries to talk Elmo into buying a country mansion. Jacko thinks she's going too far.
Jacko meets a new lady, thanks to her budgie.
The Last Of The Cybernauts...??: Steed faces the prospect of a confrontation with old foes when a vengeful double agent seeks revenge against the team.
Get-A-Way: Steed finds himself in danger when known spies escape from Oldhill Monastery. Can The Avengers locate the prisoners in time and discover how they were able to escape so freely?
Deal Me Out: Radar hits a local who is famous for jumping in front of vehicles for the compensation. Captain Pak arrives at the 4077th for a marathon poker game disguised as a conference.
Hot Lips and Empty Arms: Hot Lips receives a letter from home causing her to reassess her life; she learns her friend has married a doctor and now has a beautiful home, a pool, and children.
Officers Only: In gratitude for Hawkeye and Trapper saving his son, a general donates an officers' club to the 4077th with negative consequences. Klinger pretends to be pregnant.
Bridge of Sighs: Henry's attention turns to the prospect of love and romance when his ex-girlfriend, who has been living in America for the last thirty years, returns to the UK for a visit.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Man's Best Friend: Part 2: Alex finally meets the family of his dreams but those dreams are soon shattered when he is forced to abandon the dog he has become attached to. (S4 EP2)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Fight For Your Life: A trainer is reminded of his own shady past when a fighter he's been mentoring is asked to take a fall.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
I Was A Middle-Aged Werewolf: On Halloween night, Jonathan helps a little boy get over his fears, and in the process, get even with his older sister!
Jonathan tries to reunite a comedian and his son by getting them to star in a community play. However, the comedian's inability to take anything seriously affects their relationship.
Love: Laura's childhood friend falls in love with Mr Edwards. She doesn't seem to mind the age difference, but Mr Edwards finds it tough.
Alden's Dilemma: Mr Edwards is suspicious of a young, handsome minister sent to Walnut Grove by the diocese, and Reverend Alden feels his position is threatened.
Marvin's Garden: Jenny nearly drowns looking for a locket in the lake. Dr Marvin convinces Laura to let Jenny help with his garden to rehabilitate. She turns out to be his last patient.
The Fox: Grandpa Zeb Walton is just as surely trapped by a set of unique circumstances as a wild, young mountain animal captured by Ben Walton.
The Big Brother: John-Boy undertakes an act of charity that exposes the family to the unexpected wiles of a most unlikely con artist.
The Waiting Game: Lottery fever takes hold in Ballykissangel. Ambrose is sent on an undercover mission that could be the making of him.
Home to Roost: Deborah's mother arrives in Ormston with some surprising news. Meanwhile, Tom has some unusual advice for shopkeeper Mr Boynton.
The Earth's Sweet Being: Dr Napier starts to promote women's health issues. Some of the younger women are receptive, although she gets a frosty reception from some of the older members.
Rumpole and the Miscarriage of Justice: Rumpole agrees to defend a police officer accused of fabricating evidence during a murder trial.
Henry in Love: Family man Henry Blake returns from a trip to Tokyo, much to the relief of Hawkeye and Trapper. Whilst away he has fallen in love with an ex-cheerleader half his age.
For Want of a Boot: Hawkeye's request for boots has not come through. He leans on the supply sergeant who needs dental work and will get boots for Hawkeye if he'll talk to the camp dentist.
Operation Noselift: Private Danny Baker has a big problem and it's attached to his face. Hawkeye calls in Robbins, a plastic surgeon, to help fix the private's nose.
...the Likely Lads?: Stranger on a Train: Bob's in London to buy a wedding present, and Terry's back from Cyprus after a spell in the army. By chance, they meet on the last train home.
...the Likely Lads?: Home Is the Hero: Classic 1970s sitcom starring James Bolam, Rodney Bewes and Brigit Forsyth. Having left the army, Terry discovers that Bob is now engaged to Thelma.
...the Likely Lads?: Cold Feet: Bob is getting ready to marry Thelma, whom he's been courting since form 4B at Park Junior School. Terry's return has caused Bob to have doubts...
